side: PRO (steelman) — the strongest case TO DO the deal
rationale_for_ordering: The thesis is built before the valuation so the price cannot anchor the argument (cross-eval discipline).
vote: APPROVE — conditional on price + conditions to close

strongest_points.point: Buy beats build by ~18 months in a fast-consolidating category
The AI sequencing team + IP is the durable prize
strongest_points.evidence: 40% of new logos cite the AI feature; team is genuinely hard to replicate

strongest_points.point: NRR 109% and 74% gross margin are real and healthy
strongest_points.evidence: expansion is working; cost-of-revenue grows slower than revenue

strongest_points.point: Cross-sell into our enterprise base is incremental ARR we can actually land
Seller is motivated — ask $336M is BELOW the $390M Series C post
strongest_points.evidence: a markdown round; price is negotiable

key_assumptions: We get entry price down to <= 6.5x ARR so the return clears the 20% hurdle
Auth/tenancy tech-debt is funded day one so the cross-sell synergy can be realized
Co-founder CTO and core AI engineers are retained
Conditions to close (DPAs, IP assignments, indemnity cap) are achievable in the window
what_would_make_this_a_clear_yes: Price at ~6.0-6.5x ARR + binding retention for the AI team + the four GC conditions papered into the SPA.
where_lenses_agree: CEO + CRO + CTO all agree the capability and the AI talent are worth owning; even the CFO agrees the asset is good — the fight is only about price.
source: cross-eval steelman pass (must find the 3 strongest reasons to approve) + ceo-advisor reasoning, reconciled across the five independent lens verdicts
